cards.title: Gravity Warps Time
cards.body: According to Einstein's theory of general relativity, gravity warps the fabric of spacetime, causing time to pass slower near massive objects.

cards.title: Butterfly Wings Create Rainbows
cards.body: The microscopic scales on butterfly wings reflect light and create the beautiful colors and rainbows we see, due to a phenomenon called thin-film interference.

cards.title: Photosynthesis Powers Life
cards.body: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants, algae, and some bacteria convert sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into glucose and oxygen, supporting life on Earth.

cards.title: DNA is Highly Resilient
cards.body: DNA is an incredibly resilient molecule, able to withstand extreme temperatures, radiation, and other forms of damage, which is essential for the survival of living organisms.

cards.title: Sound Waves Have Frequency
cards.body: Sound waves have frequency, which is the number of oscillations or cycles per second, measured in Hertz, and is perceived as pitch by the human ear.

cards.title: Human Nose Can Detect Billions
cards.body: The human nose can detect an astonishing number of different odors, with estimates suggesting that we can recognize over a billion different scents.

cards.title: Black Holes Have Event Horizons
cards.body: Black holes have event horizons, which mark the boundary beyond which nothing, including light, can escape the gravitational pull of the black hole.

cards.title: Human Brain Uses 20% Energy
cards.body: The human brain accounts for only about 2% of the body's mass, but it uses approximately 20% of the body's energy expenditure, making it a highly active and energy-hungry organ.

cards.title: Earth's Atmosphere Extends Far
cards.body: The Earth's atmosphere extends far beyond what we can see, with the exosphere, the outermost layer, reaching out to about 630,000 kilometers into space.

cards.title: Chaos Theory Applies Everywhere
cards.body: Chaos theory, which describes the behavior of complex and dynamic systems, applies to a wide range of phenomena, from weather patterns to population growth and even financial markets.
